title: Google Vertex AI Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for using Google Vertex AI effectively.

commands.description: Create a custom training job in a specific region.
commands.scenario: When deploying a custom model training job on Vertex AI.

commands.description: Upload a model to Vertex AI with a display name.
commands.scenario: When you need to upload a trained model to Vertex AI for serving.

commands.description: Create an endpoint for model deployment in a specific region.
commands.scenario: When setting up an endpoint to serve predictions from your model.

commands.description: Set the randomness of the model's output.
commands.scenario: When you want a balance between creativity and determinism in model responses.

commands.description: Deploy a model to a specific endpoint.
commands.scenario: When you need to deploy a model to an endpoint for serving predictions.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Prompt technique to improve reasoning in model responses.
commands.scenario: When you need the model to provide logical and verified answers.

commands.description: List all models available in a specific region.
commands.scenario: When you need to view all models deployed in a region on Vertex AI.

commands.description: Use the GPT-4o model with streaming enabled.
commands.scenario: When you need real-time predictions from the model.

commands.description: List all endpoints in a specific region.
commands.scenario: When managing or auditing endpoints in Vertex AI.

commands.description: Describe a custom job to get details about its configuration and status.
commands.scenario: When you need to check the status or details of a custom training job.

commands.description: Delete a model from Vertex AI in a specific region.

commands.command: Use detailed examples to clarify complex concepts.
commands.description: Prompt technique to enhance understanding and clarity in model outputs.
commands.scenario: When explaining complex ideas or processes through the model.

commands.description: Delete an endpoint in a specific region.
commands.scenario: When you need to remove an endpoint that is no longer in use.

commands.description: Perform a dry-run test on a deployed endpoint using a JSON payload to validate schema before application integration.
Advanced sampling parameters: top_p controls nucleus sampling diversity, while presence_penalty reduces word repetition in long-form generation.
Execute high-throughput asynchronous batch prediction jobs on large datasets stored in Cloud Storage instead of real-time requests.

commands.command: Few-shot prompting with JSON Schema enforcement
commands.description: Pattern: Provide 3-5 input/output pairs in the prompt and explicitly define a JSON schema to force the model to output structured data.
Use gcloud filters and output formatting to programmatically retrieve only the resource ID of a model matching a specific name pattern.
Deploy a LangChain-based agent using Vertex AI Reasoning Engine (Managed Service) to orchestrate complex tool-calling workflows.
Control token sampling diversity and prevent model hallucination/runaway generation by defining explicit termination strings.
Use the --json-request flag to bypass shell escaping issues when sending complex, nested JSON payloads to Vertex AI prediction endpoints.
A prompt engineering technique where the model generates an initial response, identifies potential errors, and then performs a self-correction step to increase factual accuracy.
Explicitly specify hardware acceleration (GPUs) and machine types for custom training jobs to optimize cost and training throughput.
